steps.title: Identify the defining concept
steps.content: The passage discusses agglomeration economies—when firms cluster in one area. The example of iron/steel and cutlery firms in Wolverhampton shows related industries concentrating spatially. The blank needs a verb for this accumulation.

steps.title: Match the accumulation verb
steps.content:
Try substituting each choice literally into the sentence: "firms intercede in an area" (intervene between parties — wrong subject), "firms concur with an area" (agree with — nonsensical), "firms appeal to an area" (attract something — inverts the direction; the firms move to the area, they don't attract it). Only amass in — gather or accumulate in — correctly describes multiple firms physically concentrating in one location, which is exactly what agglomeration economies require.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
